stocks.note: Numbers as of market close April 21st.
stocks.news:
All three indexes pulled back as investors digested the Iran ceasefire extension. Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple (AAPL -2.52%) CEO this September, handing the job to hardware chief John Ternus. Cook stays on as Executive Chairman focused on global policy. The question: Apple picked a hardware lifer to lead a company whose biggest test is now AI and software.

Marvell (MRVL +2.53%) ripped on reports Google (GOOG -1.47%) may buy nearly 2 million of Marvell's new memory units plus a new inference TPU. Google's long relied on Broadcom chips, but is branching out to win the AI race.

economy.note: US 10-Year and Gold as of 4:00 PM ET April 21st; WTI Crude reflects today's intraday move via investing.com.
economy.commentary:
Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh had his first Congressional hearing. He refused to promise a rate cut, said he'll divest about $100M of assets within his first 90 days, and Senator Thom Tillis is blocking his vote until the DOJ finishes probing current Chair Jerome Powell. Powell's last day is slated for May 15th.

Retail sales jumped 1.7% in March, the biggest monthly gain in three years. Gas stations drove most of that as oil prices spiked on the Iran war. Americans spent more even as food and gas went up. Note the split from earlier consumer sentiment reads hitting record lows.

Tariff refund drama: Trump said he'll "remember" companies that claim refunds on the Supreme-Court-killed tariffs. $160B is due to be refunded. Levi (LEVI +1.08%) expects $80M. Apple and Amazon may pass rather than invite scrutiny.

crypto.note: Numbers as of 4:00 PM ET on April 21st.
crypto.commentary:
Bitcoin nudged higher as the ceasefire extension calmed geopolitics. Biggest hack of 2026 so far: a hacker stole 116,500 rsETH worth $292M through Kelp DAO's LayerZero bridge platform.

Americans can now trade crypto futures on prediction market Polymarket, and Kalshi is launching a similar product later this month. Both now compete directly with Coinbase (COIN -7.41%), Robinhood (HOOD -5.31%), and Gemini (GEMI -3.64%) on one of crypto's most profitable products. Kalshi's monthly crypto volume already tops $1B. Polymarket's weekly notional passed $1B in Q1.

real_estate.homebuilder: Rates holding steady at 6.33% with MBS prices slightly higher this morning, which may help rates tick down a touch by close. The Iran ceasefire got extended two weeks. A small win for rate stability. But the Strait of Hormuz keeps opening and closing, which is why oil is back at $93.
real_estate.commentary:
Total active inventory across the 15-city board is 2,691, down slightly from 2,705 yesterday. Fresh supply today came in at 219 new listings, meaningfully lower than yesterday's 266. A one-day dip but worth watching.

📍 Alameda & Contra Costa Snapshot (04/22/26)

📍 Fremont: 29 new. Cooler fresh supply than yesterday's 47.

📍 Union City: 7 new. Still one of the tightest markets on the board.

📍 Newark: 4 new. Light stock flow.

📍 Hayward: 21 new. Leading the I-880 corridor again.

📍 Milpitas: 4 new. Pending 29 townhomes. Absorption still strong.

📍 Pleasanton: 17 new. Tri-Valley steady.

📍 Dublin: 17 new. Moderate.

📍 San Ramon: 20 new. Balanced.

📍 Livermore: 11 new. Softer than yesterday's 22.

📍 Castro Valley: 9 new. Quiet.

📍 Danville: 16 new. Contra Costa cooling from yesterday's 26.

📍 San Leandro: 10 new. Steady.

📍 San Lorenzo: 4 new. Minimal.

📍 Oakland: 50 new. Still the volume leader.

Senior housing is quietly the biggest housing story of 2026. REITs Welltower (WELL -1.46%), Ventas (VTR -3.00%), and Healthpeak (DOC -3.68%) have invested billions into senior housing. Occupancy hit a record 89.1% in 2025 and could cross 90% this year. Why it matters: Boomers turning 80 means more downsizing inventory coming to market, and buyers competing for senior-living units will bump against institutional capital. For our Fremont and Tri-Valley agents, this is the listing conversation with parents-in-law and longtime owners who have been sitting on equity.

For listing agents: Fresh supply cooled today. If you have a listing with strong pending interest, don't be shy about tightening review dates. Buyer urgency is real when inventory dips, even on a one-day sample.

Retail is also returning. American Eagle (AEO -2.87%) just signed a 14,000 sq ft contract for multiple stores in Chicago, and local brokerage Kirsh Agency projects Magnificent Mile deal volume will double in 2026. Not a Bay Area headline but a signal that commercial landlords are finally finding bottom.
